paragraph:
# Velmoria's archive

*One hundred and sixty-five years on, a dispute over the national memory tests whether a free people can govern even their own past*

LET THIS chapter turn on a question of memory. One hundred and sixty-five years after the marshal fell, Velmoria faced a quarrel not over power or money but over the past itself—a dispute over what to do with the vast national archive, the (locus) of the republic's collective memory. It was a (heterogeneous) trove: the marshal's own papers, the (muniments) of the founding, the (oddments) and (artifacts) of a century and a half of self-rule. The (onus) of deciding its fate fell, fittingly, on the people.

The archive had grown to a (gross) (excess) of material—a (surplus) so vast that no single (caucus) could (assess) it. Some of it was (peerless): the original constitution, the founders' letters, the (lineaments) of the republic's birth. Some was (dross): the (feckless) propaganda of the marshal's regime, his (bogus) decrees, the (artless) flattery of his court. The question was what to preserve, what to (toss), and who should decide. It was a small question with a (tremendous) shadow.

A faction urged a great (exodus) of the marshal's papers—to compel their (egress) from the archive entirely, to purge the (locus) of memory of his taint. His records were (hazardous), they argued, a (stimulus) to nostalgia, an (incubus) on the national conscience. Better to be (ruthless) and (relentless)—to give the tyrant's memory the (double-cross), erasing the record he had trusted would outlive him—than to let the (dross) of tyranny (oppress) the present. Their (animus) was understandable; their (assertiveness), even (admirable). But their proposal was, in the end, a kind of (regress).

The opposing view was made with (judiciousness). To destroy the marshal's records, however (gross) their content, would be its own (transgress)ion—a (heavy-handedness) that mirrored his own. A republic that could not bear to keep the (artifacts) of its darkest hour was a republic afraid of its own memory. The (onus) of freedom, this faction held, includes the duty to remember without flinching. To preserve the (dross) alongside the (peerless) was not mere (positiveness)—a facile optimism—but a (judiciousness) born of strength.

Recall how the marshal himself had treated memory. He was (ruthless) in his (unscrupulousness)—a (relentless) editor of the past who would (emboss) his own legend and (gloss) over his crimes. He kept his archive as a (locus) of lies, ordering the (exodus) of every inconvenient record, the (egress) of every honest (artifact). His historians were (spineless) and (feckless); his (caucus) of flatterers (artless) in their praise. He would (double-cross) the very notion of truth, treating the national memory as his to (emboss) or (toss) at will. To him the past was a (stylus) for writing his own apotheosis.

The republic, (chasten)ed by that memory, refused both the (ruthless) purge and the (feckless) hoard. It convened a national commission—historians, archivists, ordinary citizens, mingling with an easy (gregariousness)—to (assess) the archive with (judiciousness) rather than (animus). Their charge was not to sit in (oppress)ive judgment over the past, nor to (gloss) it, but to organize it: to preserve the (peerless) and the (dross) alike, to label the (bogus) as (bogus) and the (gross) as (gross), and to make the whole (heterogeneous) trove accessible to any citizen who wished to (press) an inquiry into it.

The work proceeded with neither (leisureliness) nor (restless) haste, but with patient, steady purpose. The commission sorted the (oddments) and (muniments), catalogued the (artifacts), and built from the (gross) (surplus) a coherent record. They did not (digress) into (excess) or lapse into (listless) delay. Their reports had a (pithiness) to match their economy of purpose; they were neither (stingy) with resources nor (heavy-handed) in judgment. The marshal's papers were preserved—not (emboss)ed in honor, but kept as evidence, a (stimulus) to vigilance rather than nostalgia.

There were, of course, those who tried to make (mischief). A (shiftless) populist saw in the archive dispute a chance to (press) his own advantage. He railed that the commission was (spineless) for keeping the marshal's records, then (ruthless) for not keeping them all—a (double-cross) of (artless) opportunism. His positions were (heterogeneous) to the point of incoherence, a (rebus) no honest citizen could (decipher). He sought to (oppress) the debate with (testiness) and (oafishness), to turn a (judicious) question into a (fracas). But the public, in whom a century and a half of vigilance had hardened into settled (mores), saw through his (shiftiness). His (animus) found no (locus) to take root.

The resolution was a quiet triumph of (judiciousness). The archive would be preserved whole—the (peerless) and the (dross), the founding (muniments) and the marshal's (bogus) decrees—and opened to all. A new wing was built on the (outskirts) of the capital, near the old (isthmus) road, to house it. The marshal's papers were placed not in a (necropolis) of honor but in a plain, well-lit reading room, where any citizen could (assess) them and draw their own conclusions. The past would neither (oppress) the present nor be erased by it.

Beyond the archive dispute, ordinary life kept its (bounteous) round. A farmer harvested (asparagus) from his (herbaceous) beds; a cook trimmed the woody (terminus) of each stalk; a child studied the (radius) of a circle and the (genus) of a beetle for school. A (curvaceous) cloud—a great (cumulus)—drifted over the coast; a (hippopotamus) (the zoo's improbable star) wallowed in its pool while children pressed against the rail. A glassblower shaped a (vitreous), (gorgeous) vase; a butcher trimmed the (osseous) waste from his cuts; a carpenter swept the (ligneous) shavings from his bench; a geologist showed students the difference between (igneous) and sedimentary rock. There was a (spontaneous), (simultaneous) ease to it all—the (gregariousness) of a free people at peace with their history.

The episode entered the national (annals) as a lesson in governing memory. A republic, the historians wrote, is tested not only by how it wields power but by how it keeps its past—whether it has the (judiciousness) to preserve even the (dross) without being (oppress)ed by it, the (assertiveness) to label lies as lies without the (heavy-handedness) of erasure. To (toss) the marshal's records would have been a (feckless) comfort; to (emboss) them, a (gross) error. The republic chose the harder, (peerless) path: to remember everything, judge honestly, and let each citizen (press) their own inquiry into the truth.

So Velmoria, one hundred and sixty-five years free, learns that a free people must govern even their own memory—neither (gloss)ing the past nor purging it in (excess), but preserving the whole (heterogeneous) record with (judiciousness) and (candor). The marshal ruled by the (unscrupulousness) of a (relentless) editor, embossing his legend and tossing the truth; his country, a century and a half and more past his fall, has chosen the (converse)—to keep the (dross) beside the (peerless), to face its own (artifacts) without (animus) or (duress), and to treat the national archive not as a (locus) of (oppress)ion but as a (bounteous), open record, where a free people may forever (assess) where they came from, the better to govern where they go.

quiz.question: _____ recess
quiz.explanation: 课间休息; 休会, 午餐休息. Children play outside during lunch recess. 孩子们在午餐休息期间在外面玩耍。 | re->回，cess->走，recess->退回去的间隙=休息/凹处（cf. recession退潮，access进入，concession让步）

quiz.question: excess _______
quiz.explanation: 过度; 超额, 超重行李. Airlines charge fees for excess baggage. 航空公司对超重行李收费。 | ex->向外，cess->走，excess->走出去超过=超额（cf. exceed超过，excessive过度的，access进入）

quiz.question: confess _____
quiz.explanation: 承认; 坦白, 承认罪行. The suspect refused to confess guilt to the crime. 嫌疑人拒绝承认罪行。 | con->共同，fess->说（拉丁fateri->承认），confess->当众承认=坦白（cf. professor教授=公开宣称，manifest明显的）

quiz.question: ________ largess
quiz.explanation: 慷慨赠予; 施舍, 慷慨赠予. The billionaires generous largess funded the new library. 亿万富翁的慷慨赠予资助了新图书馆。 | large->大，largess->大手笔的赠予=慷慨赠予（cf. large大，enlarge扩大，largesse法语拼法）

quiz.question: guileless _____
quiz.explanation: 天真的; 坦率的, 天真的孩子. The guileless child answered every question honestly. 天真的孩子诚实地回答每个问题。 | guile->欺骗（古法语），less->没有，guileless->没有欺骗的=天真的（cf. guile诡计，beguile迷惑）

quiz.question: spineless ______
quiz.explanation: 没有骨气的; 软弱的, 软弱的懦夫. The spineless coward refused to stand up for his beliefs. 软弱的懦夫拒绝为自己的信念挺身而出。 | spine->脊椎/骨气，less->没有，spineless->没有脊椎=没有骨气的（cf. spine脊椎，spinal脊椎的）

quiz.question: feckless __________
quiz.explanation: 无能的; 软弱的, 无能的领导. The company suffered under feckless leadership. 公司在无能的领导下遭受损失。 | feck->效果（苏格兰语，effect的方言），less->没有，feckless->没有效果=无能的（cf. effect效果，feckful有能力的）

quiz.question: peerless ______
quiz.explanation: 无与伦比的; 卓越的, 无与伦比的美丽. She possessed peerless beauty that captivated everyone. 她拥有无与伦比的美丽,迷住了所有人。 | peer->同等者（拉丁par），less->没有，peerless->没有同等者=无与伦比的（cf. peer同龄人，appear出现，compare比较）

quiz.question: shiftless _____
quiz.explanation: 懒散的; 无进取心的, 懒散的懒汉. The shiftless idler refused to find work. 懒散的懒汉拒绝找工作。 | shift->改变/变通，less->没有，shiftless->没有变通能力=懒散无进取心的（cf. shift换班，makeshift临时的）

quiz.question: relentless _______
quiz.explanation: 无情的; 不懈的, 不懈的追求. The detective showed relentless pursuit of the truth. 侦探对真相进行了不懈的追求。 | re->回，lent->放松（拉丁lentus），less->不，relentless->不会放松的=不懈的（cf. relent软化，lento缓慢的）

quiz.question: bootless ______
quiz.explanation: 无益的; 徒劳的, 徒劳的努力. Their bootless effort yielded no results. 他们徒劳的努力没有产生任何结果。 | boot->好处（古英语bot->补救），less->没有，bootless->没有好处的=徒劳的（cf. boots无益，to boot额外地）

quiz.question: artless _____
quiz.explanation: 天真的; 自然的, 天然的魅力. Her artless charm made her very likable. 她天然的魅力使她非常讨人喜欢。 | art->技巧，less->没有，artless->没有技巧修饰的=天真自然的（cf. artful狡猾/有技巧的，artifact人工制品）

quiz.question: restless _____
quiz.explanation: 不安的; 焦躁的, 不安的夜晚. He spent a restless night worrying about the exam. 他度过了一个不安的夜晚,为考试担心。 | rest->休息，less->没有，restless->无法休息的=焦躁不安的（cf. rest休息，arrest逮捕=停止）

quiz.question: listless _____
quiz.explanation: 无精打采的; 倦怠的, 无精打采的人群. The listless crowd showed little enthusiasm. 无精打采的人群几乎没有热情。 | list->欲望/倾向（古英语lust），less->没有，listless->没有欲望的=无精打采的（cf. lust欲望，listless同根）

quiz.question: gutless ______
quiz.explanation: 胆小的; 懦弱的, 胆小的懦夫. The gutless coward fled at the first sign of danger. 胆小的懦夫一看到危险就逃跑了。 | gut->内脏/勇气（口语），less->没有，gutless->没有勇气的=胆小的（cf. gut直觉/勇气，gutsy有胆量的）

quiz.question: ____________ heavy-handedness
quiz.explanation: 笨拙; 粗暴, 官僚主义的粗暴. The bureaucratic heavy-handedness frustrated citizens. 官僚主义的粗暴让市民感到沮丧。 | heavy-hand->重手，ness->状态，heavy-handedness->用重手处理的状态=粗暴/强硬

quiz.question: ____ positiveness
quiz.explanation: 肯定; 确信, 坚定的肯定. She spoke with firm positiveness about her decision. 她对自己的决定表现出坚定的肯定。 | positive->肯定的（拉丁positivus->确定），positiveness->确定性（cf. positive积极的，deposit存款）

quiz.question: _______ assertiveness
quiz.explanation: 自信; 果断, 健康的自信. Healthy assertiveness helps in professional settings. 健康的自信在职业环境中很有帮助。 | assert->宣称（拉丁asserere->加入/声明），assertiveness->自我主张的能力（cf. assert断言，dissertation论文）

quiz.question: _________ restiveness
quiz.explanation: 不安; 焦躁, 政治动荡. Political restiveness grew among the populace. 民众中的政治动荡加剧了。 | restive->不安分的（原意是拒绝前进的马），restiveness->躁动不安（cf. restless无法休息，restive拒绝服从）

quiz.question: oafishness ___ _______
quiz.explanation: 粗鲁; 笨拙, 粗鲁和虚张声势. His oafishness and bluster alienated colleagues. 他的粗鲁和虚张声势疏远了同事。 | oaf->笨蛋（来自elf精灵，被精灵换掉的愚笨孩子），oafishness->笨拙粗鲁（cf. oafish愚蠢的）

quiz.question: _____ stinginess
quiz.explanation: 吝啬; 小气, 彻底的吝啬. His sheer stinginess prevented him from helping others. 他彻底的吝啬阻止了他帮助别人。 | sting->刺（象声词），stingy->像蜇刺一样不肯放开钱=吝啬的，stinginess->吝啬（cf. sting刺，stringent严格的）

quiz.question: pithiness __ _____
quiz.explanation: 简洁; 精炼, 风格的简洁. The pithiness of style made the essay effective. 风格的简洁使文章很有效。 | pith->髓/精髓（古英语），pithy->精炼有力的，pithiness->简洁有力（cf. pith果皮髓，pithy言简意赅）

quiz.question: tackiness __ ____
quiz.explanation: 粘性; 俗气, 胶水的粘性. Test the tackiness of glue before applying. 在使用前测试胶水的粘性。 | tack->短钉/粘性（象声词），tacky->粘的/俗气的，tackiness->粘性/俗气（cf. thumbtack图钉，tack策略）

quiz.question: leisureliness ____
quiz.explanation: 悠闲; 从容, 悠闲的步调. They walked at a leisureliness pace through the park. 他们以悠闲的步调穿过公园。 | leisure->闲暇（古法语leisir->被允许），leisurely->从容的，leisureliness->悠然自在（cf. leisure闲暇，leisurely悠闲地）

quiz.question: _________ shiftiness
quiz.explanation: 狡猾; 诡诈, 政治上的狡猾. Voters distrusted the candidates political shiftiness. 选民不信任候选人政治上的狡猾。 | shift->转变/诡计，shifty->狡猾的，shiftiness->狡猾（cf. shift换班，shiftless懒散的）

quiz.question: testiness __ _____
quiz.explanation: 易怒; 暴躁, 声音中的易怒. The testiness in voice revealed his frustration. 声音中的易怒显示了他的沮丧。 | testy->易怒的（来自头部/头脑过热），testiness->易怒的性格（cf. testy暴躁，protest抗议）

quiz.question: _____ harness
quiz.explanation: 马具; 利用, 马具. The farmer adjusted the horse harness before plowing. 农民在犁地前调整了马具。 | harness->装备（古挪威hernest->军队补给），harness->马具/利用（in harness->在岗工作，cf. harness energy利用能源）

quiz.question: ____ ruthlessness
quiz.explanation: 无情; 冷酷, 冷酷无情. His cold ruthlessness in business was well known. 他在商业上的冷酷无情是众所周知的。 | ruth->怜悯（古英语hreowan），less->没有，ruthless->没有怜悯=无情的（cf. ruthless无情的，rueful懊悔的）

quiz.question: judiciousness ___ _________
quiz.explanation: 明智; 审慎, 明智和克制. The judge showed judiciousness and restraint in sentencing. 法官在量刑时表现出明智和克制。 | judici->判断（拉丁judex->法官），judicious->明智的，judiciousness->明智（cf. judge法官，judicial司法的）

quiz.question: gregariousness _____
quiz.explanation: 合群; 社交性, 合群特质. Her gregariousness trait made her popular at parties. 她的合群特质使她在聚会上很受欢迎。 | greg->群（拉丁grex->羊群），gregarious->喜欢群居的，gregariousness->合群（cf. aggregate聚集，segregate隔离）

quiz.question: unscrupulousness __ ________
quiz.explanation: 不择手段; 无道德原则, 商业上的不择手段. His unscrupulousness in business led to lawsuits. 他商业上的不择手段导致了诉讼。 | scrupulus->小石子/顾虑（拉丁），unscrupulous->不在乎顾虑的=不择手段（cf. scruple顾虑，scrupulous谨慎的）

quiz.question: ________ fastness
quiz.explanation: 要塞; 堡垒, 山地要塞. Rebels retreated to their mountain fastness. 叛军撤退到他们的山地要塞。 | fast->固定/坚固（古英语fæst），fastness->坚固之地=要塞（cf. steadfast坚定，fasten系紧，fast斋戒/快速）

quiz.question: ____ redress
quiz.explanation: 纠正; 补救, 寻求补救. Victims have the right to seek redress in court. 受害者有权在法庭上寻求补救。 | re->重新，dress->整理，redress->重新整理=纠正补救（cf. address处理，dress穿着/整理，redress grievances纠正不公）

quiz.question: _________ egress
quiz.explanation: 出口; 外出, 紧急出口. All buildings must have an emergency egress route. 所有建筑物必须有紧急出口路线。 | e->向外，gress->走，egress->走出去=出口（cf. ingress进入，progress进步，digress偏题）

quiz.question: ________ regress
quiz.explanation: 退步; 倒退, 经济倒退. The country experienced economic regress during the crisis. 该国在危机期间经历了经济倒退。 | re->回，gress->走，regress->往回走=倒退（cf. progress前进，congress会议，transgress越界）

quiz.question: digress ____ _____
quiz.explanation: 离题; 偏离, 离题. The speaker would often digress from topic unnecessarily. 演讲者经常不必要地离题。 | di->分开，gress->走，digress->走偏=离题（cf. progress进步，regress倒退，digression离题）

quiz.question: _____ __ ingress
quiz.explanation: 进入; 入口, 进入点. Security monitored every point of ingress to the building. 保安监控着建筑物的每个进入点。 | in->进入，gress->走，ingress->走进去=入口（cf. egress出口，congress共同走=会议）

quiz.question: transgress ___
quiz.explanation: 违反; 越界, 违反法律. Those who transgress law will face consequences. 违反法律的人将面临后果。 | trans->跨越，gress->走，transgress->越过界限=违反（cf. trespass侵犯，transgression违法）

quiz.question: ____ press
quiz.explanation: 新闻界; 压, 自由新闻界. A free press is essential to democracy. 自由新闻界对民主至关重要。 | press->压/新闻（拉丁premere->压），press->压迫/新闻媒体（cf. pressure压力，impress使印象深刻，express表达）

quiz.question: oppress __________
quiz.explanation: 压迫; 欺压, 压迫少数群体. Dictators often oppress minorities in their countries. 独裁者经常压迫本国的少数群体。 | op->朝向，press->压，oppress->从上方压制=压迫（cf. suppress压制，repress镇压，depress使沮丧）

quiz.question: _________ distress
quiz.explanation: 痛苦; 困境, 财务困境. The company faced severe financial distress. 公司面临严重的财务困境。 | dis->分离/使难，stress->紧张，distress->造成极大紧张=痛苦（cf. stress压力，stress强调，distress signal求救信号）

quiz.question: _____ buttress
quiz.explanation: 扶壁; 支撑, 石扶壁. The cathedrals stone buttress supported the walls. 大教堂的石扶壁支撑着墙壁。 | butt->推顶（古法语boter），buttress->用来推顶支撑的结构=扶壁（cf. butt撞击，abutment桥台）

quiz.question: _____ duress
quiz.explanation: 胁迫; 强制, 在胁迫下. He signed the contract under duress. 他在胁迫下签署了合同。 | durus->坚硬/严酷（拉丁），duress->严酷强迫=胁迫（cf. durable耐久的，endure忍耐，dour阴郁的）

quiz.question: assess ____
quiz.explanation: 评估; 估价, 评估风险. Investors must carefully assess risk before investing. 投资者在投资前必须仔细评估风险。 | as->朝向，sess->坐（拉丁sedere），assess->坐在旁边评估=评估（cf. session会议，possess拥有，obsess痴迷）

quiz.question: ____ bliss
quiz.explanation: 极乐; 幸福, 纯粹的幸福. The vacation was pure bliss from start to finish. 从头到尾,这个假期都是纯粹的幸福。 | bliss->极乐（古英语bliths->快乐），blissful->幸福的（cf. bless祝福，bliss=bless的名词，blissful ignorance幸福的无知）

quiz.question: remiss __ ____
quiz.explanation: 疏忽的; 玩忽职守的, 玩忽职守. The guard was remiss in duty during the break-in. 警卫在入室盗窃期间玩忽职守。 | re->回，miss->放开（拉丁mittere->送），remiss->放松了不该放松的=疏忽（cf. remit汇款，emit发射，submit提交）

quiz.question: emboss _ ____
quiz.explanation: 压印; 使凸出, 压印标志. The company will emboss a logo on the leather goods. 公司将在皮革制品上压印标志。 | em->使，boss->凸出/老板，emboss->使凸出=压印浮雕（cf. boss老板=凸出的领导者，deboss凹雕）

quiz.question: gloss ____

quiz.question: villainous ____
quiz.explanation: 恶棍的; 邪恶的, 邪恶的行为. The criminal committed a villainous deed that shocked everyone. 罪犯犯下了震惊所有人的邪恶行为。 | villain->农奴/恶棍（古法语vilain->乡下人），villainous->恶棍般邪恶的（cf. villa别墅，village村庄，villain原指乡下粗人）

quiz.question: mountainous _______
quiz.explanation: 多山的, 多山的地形. The rescue team navigated the mountainous terrain carefully. 救援队小心地穿越多山的地形。 | mountain->山（古法语montaigne），mountainous->充满山地的=多山的（cf. mount攀登，surmount克服，paramount最重要的）

quiz.question: tendinous ______
quiz.explanation: 腱的, 肌腱损伤. The athlete suffered a tendinous injury during training. 运动员在训练中遭受了肌腱损伤。 | tendin->腱（拉丁tendere->伸展），tendinous->肌腱的（cf. tendon肌腱，tenacious顽强，tender温柔/投标）

quiz.question: vicissitudinous ____
quiz.explanation: 变化无常的; 多灾多难的, 变化无常的生活. He led a vicissitudinous life full of ups and downs. 他过着充满起起落落的变化无常的生活。 | vicissit->交替变化（拉丁vicis->轮换），vicissitudinous->充满变化的（cf. vice反面，vicarious替代的，vicissitude沧桑）

quiz.question: heinous _____
quiz.explanation: 极恶的; 令人发指的, 令人发指的罪行. The judge sentenced the perpetrator of the heinous crime. 法官对令人发指的罪行的犯罪者进行了判决。 | hain->憎恨（古法语haineus->令人憎恨），heinous->令人憎恨的=极其邪恶的（cf. hate憎恨，heinousness极恶）

quiz.question: coterminous _______
quiz.explanation: 毗连的; 同时发生的, 毗连的边界. The two countries share coterminous borders. 这两个国家有毗连的边界。 | co->共同，termin->边界（拉丁terminus），coterminous->共享边界的=毗连的（cf. terminate终止，terminal终端，terminus终点）

quiz.question: voluminous _____
quiz.explanation: 大量的; 卷帙浩繁的, 大量的笔记. The historian left behind voluminous notes on the period. 历史学家留下了关于那个时期的大量笔记。 | volum->卷（拉丁volumen->卷轴），voluminous->像卷轴一样多的=大量的（cf. volume卷/音量，revolve旋转，evolve进化）

quiz.question: numinous ________
quiz.explanation: 神圣的; 超自然的, 神圣的存在. The ancient temple had a numinous presence. 古老的寺庙有一种神圣的存在感。 | numen->神力（拉丁），numinous->有神圣力量的（cf. numen神明意志，innumerable无数的，numeral数字=神定的数量）

quiz.question: cacophonous _____
quiz.explanation: 刺耳的; 不和谐的, 嘈杂的人群. The cacophonous crowd made it impossible to hear. 嘈杂的人群使人无法听清。 | cac->坏（希腊kakos），phon->声音，cacophonous->声音难听的=刺耳的（cf. euphonious悦耳的，symphony交响乐，cacophony噪音）

quiz.question: synchronous ______
quiz.explanation: 同步的; 同时发生的, 同步时钟. The synchronous clocks displayed the same time exactly. 同步时钟准确显示相同的时间。 | syn->共同，chron->时间（希腊），synchronous->同时间的=同步的（cf. chronicle年代记，chronic慢性的，anachronism时代错误）

quiz.question: monotonous ____
quiz.explanation: 单调的; 乏味的, 单调的语气. His monotonous tone put the audience to sleep. 他单调的语气让观众昏昏欲睡。 | mono->单一，ton->声音（希腊），monotonous->只有单一声调的=单调的（cf. monotone单调，monologue独白，monopoly垄断）

quiz.question: gluttonous _____
quiz.explanation: 暴食的; 贪吃的, 暴食的食客. The gluttonous eater consumed an enormous amount. 暴食的食客吃了大量食物。 | glutton->贪吃者（拉丁gluttire->吞咽），gluttonous->暴食的（cf. gulp大口吞咽，swallow吞，gluttony暴食=七宗罪之一）

quiz.question: pompous ______
quiz.explanation: 自大的; 浮夸的, 浮夸的演讲. His pompous speech failed to impress the audience. 他浮夸的演讲未能打动观众。 | pomp->盛大游行（希腊pompe），pompous->喜欢大肆排场的=自大浮夸的（cf. pomp排场，pomposity自大，pompon绒球）

quiz.question: acarpous _____
quiz.explanation: 不结果实的, 不结果实的植物. The acarpous plant produces only flowers, no fruit. 不结果实的植物只开花不结果。 | a->无，carp->果实（希腊karpos），acarpous->无果实的（cf. carpel心皮，pericarp果皮，cf. Carpe Diem及时行乐）

quiz.question: barbarous ___
quiz.explanation: 野蛮的; 残暴的, 野蛮的行为. The soldiers committed barbarous act during the war. 士兵在战争期间犯下了野蛮的行为。 | barbaros->外国人（希腊，模仿外国人说话声"bar bar"），barbarous->像野蛮外族的（cf. barbarian野蛮人，barb倒钩）

quiz.question: scabrous _______
quiz.explanation: 粗糙的; 猥亵的, 粗糙的表面. The old wood had a scabrous surface covered in splinters. 旧木头有一个布满碎片的粗糙表面。 | scab->结痂（拉丁scabere->刮），scabrous->表面粗糙如痂的（cf. scab罢工破坏者，scabies疥疮，rough粗糙）

quiz.question: anhydrous ____
quiz.explanation: 无水的, 无水盐. The chemist used anhydrous salt in the experiment. 化学家在实验中使用了无水盐。 | an->无，hydr->水（希腊），anhydrous->不含水的（cf. hydrate水合物，dehydrate脱水，hydrogen氢=产生水的元素）

quiz.question: slanderous _____
quiz.explanation: 诽谤的; 中伤的, 诽谤性谣言. The victim sued over the slanderous rumor spread online. 受害者因网上传播的诽谤性谣言提起诉讼。 | slander->口头诽谤（古法语esclandre->丑闻），slanderous->诽谤的（cf. scandal丑闻，libelous书面诽谤，slander口头诽谤）

quiz.question: ponderous _____
quiz.explanation: 笨重的; 沉闷的, 沉闷的散文. The authors ponderous prose made reading difficult. 作者沉闷的散文使阅读变得困难。 | ponder->称重/思考（拉丁ponderare），ponderous->沉重难以移动的（cf. ponder思考，pound磅=重量，preponderant占优势的）

quiz.question: saliferous _______
quiz.explanation: 含盐的, 含盐矿床. Miners discovered a large saliferous deposit underground. 矿工在地下发现了一个大型含盐矿床。 | sal->盐（拉丁sal），fer->携带，saliferous->携带盐分的=含盐的（cf. salary工资=古代用盐支付，salad沙拉=加盐的蔬菜）

quiz.question: odoriferous ____
quiz.explanation: 有气味的; 芳香的, 芳香的草药. The garden grew various odoriferous herb species. 花园种植了各种芳香的草药。 | odor->气味（拉丁），fer->携带，odoriferous->散发气味的=芳香的（cf. odor气味，deodorant除臭剂，olfaction嗅觉）

quiz.question: cantankerous ___ ___
quiz.explanation: 脾气坏的; 爱争吵的, 脾气坏的老人. The cantankerous old man complained about everything. 脾气坏的老人对每件事都抱怨。 | cantankerous->喜争吵（来源不明，可能来自conteck->争吵），cantankerous->脾气暴躁爱吵架的（cf. contentious有争议的）

quiz.question: numerous ________
quiz.explanation: 许多的; 大量的, 许多例子. The textbook provides numerous examples of the concept. 教科书提供了该概念的许多例子。 | numer->数（拉丁numerus），numerous->数量多的（cf. number数字，enumerate列举，innumerable无数的）

quiz.question: prosperous ___
quiz.explanation: 繁荣的; 兴旺的, 繁荣时代. The country enjoyed a prosperous era of growth. 该国享受了一个繁荣的增长时代。 | pro->向前，sper->希望（拉丁sperare），prosperous->朝希望的方向走=繁荣的（cf. despair绝望，prosper繁荣，prosperity繁荣）

quiz.question: preposterous ____
quiz.explanation: 荒谬的; 可笑的, 荒谬的想法. His plan was a preposterous idea that no one supported. 他的计划是一个没人支持的荒谬想法。 | pre->前，post->后，preposterous->把前面的放后面=本末倒置=荒谬的（cf. preposition前置词，posterior后部，前后颠倒）

quiz.question: dexterous _____
quiz.explanation: 灵巧的; 熟练的, 灵巧的双手. The surgeons dexterous hands performed the delicate operation. 外科医生灵巧的双手进行了精细的手术。 | dexter->右手（拉丁，右手被认为灵巧），dexterous->右手般灵巧的（cf. ambidextrous两手均灵，sinister左/邪恶，dexterity灵巧）

quiz.question: desirous __
quiz.explanation: 渴望的; 想要的, 渴望. She was desirous of achieving success in her career. 她渴望在事业上取得成功。 | desir->渴望（拉丁desiderare->思念星星），desirous->充满渴望的（cf. desire渴望，desiderate渴望，consider考虑=与星星同在）

quiz.question: chivalrous ______
quiz.explanation: 侠义的; 有骑士风度的, 侠义的骑士. The chivalrous knight protected the innocent. 侠义的骑士保护无辜者。 | cheval->马（法语），chivalrous->骑马的=有骑士风度的（cf. cavalry骑兵，cavalier骑士/轻慢的，chivalry骑士精神）

quiz.question: vigorous ______
quiz.explanation: 有力的; 精力充沛的, 激烈的辩论. The candidates engaged in vigorous debate on stage. 候选人在台上进行了激烈的辩论。 | vigor->活力（拉丁vigor），vigorous->充满活力的（cf. vigor活力，invigorate使活跃，vigil守夜=保持活跃）

quiz.question: valorous ____
quiz.explanation: 英勇的; 勇敢的, 英勇的行为. The soldier performed a valorous deed in battle. 士兵在战斗中做出了英勇的行为。 | valor->勇气（拉丁valere->强壮），valorous->有勇气的=英勇的（cf. valor英勇，valid有效，value价值，valiant勇敢的）

quiz.question: dolorous ____
quiz.explanation: 悲伤的; 令人痛苦的, 悲伤的消息. The family received dolorous news about their father. 家人收到了关于父亲的悲伤消息。 | dolor->痛苦（拉丁dolor），dolorous->充满痛苦的（cf. doleful悲伤的，condolences哀悼，dolor痛苦）

quiz.question: glamorous ____
quiz.explanation: 迷人的; 富有魅力的, 迷人的生活. The actress lived a glamorous life in Hollywood. 这位女演员在好莱坞过着迷人的生活。 | glamour->魔法（苏格兰语grammar的变体，中世纪知识=魔法），glamorous->有魔力迷人的（cf. grammar语法，glamour魔法/魅力）

quiz.question: timorous ______
quiz.explanation: 胆怯的; 胆小的, 胆怯的一瞥. She cast a timorous glance at the intimidating figure. 她向那个令人生畏的人物投去胆怯的一瞥。 | timor->恐惧（拉丁timere），timorous->充满恐惧的=胆怯的（cf. timid胆小，intimidate恐吓，Timor帝汶岛=令人恐惧的）

quiz.question: sonorous _____
quiz.explanation: 洪亮的; 响亮的, 洪亮的声音. The speakers sonorous voice filled the auditorium. 演讲者洪亮的声音充满了礼堂。 | sonor->声音（拉丁sonare），sonorous->声音洪亮的（cf. sonic声音的，resonant共鸣的，sound声音，sonar声呐）

quiz.question: porous ____
quiz.explanation: 多孔的; 能渗透的, 多孔岩石. Water easily flows through porous rock formations. 水很容易流过多孔岩石层。 | por->孔（拉丁porus），porous->多孔的（cf. pore毛孔，porosityI孔隙率，impervious不可渗透的）

quiz.question: vaporous ____
quiz.explanation: 模糊的; 空洞的, 模糊的想法. His plan was just a vaporous idea with no substance. 他的计划只是一个没有实质内容的模糊想法。 | vapor->蒸气（拉丁vapor），vaporous->像蒸气一样飘渺的=空洞的（cf. evaporate蒸发，vaporize气化，vapor trail飞机云迹）

quiz.question: nonporous _______
quiz.explanation: 无孔的; 不透水的, 无孔表面. The countertop has a nonporous surface that resists stains. 台面有一个抗污渍的无孔表面。 | non->无，por->孔，nonporous->没有孔的（cf. porous多孔，impervious不透水，cf. sealed surface密封表面）

quiz.question: herbivorous ____
quiz.explanation: 食草的, 食草性饮食. The elephant follows a herbivorous diet of plants. 大象遵循植物的食草性饮食。 | herb->草，vor->吃（拉丁vorare），herbivorous->吃草的（cf. carnivorous食肉，omnivorous杂食，voracious贪吃的）

quiz.question: omnivorous ____
quiz.explanation: 杂食的, 杂食性饮食. Bears maintain an omnivorous diet of plants and meat. 熊保持植物和肉类的杂食性饮食。 | omni->全部，vor->吃，omnivorous->什么都吃的（cf. omnipotent全能，omniscient全知，omnibus公共汽车=载所有人）

quiz.question: carnivorous _____
quiz.explanation: 食肉的, 食肉植物. The Venus flytrap is a carnivorous plant that eats insects. 捕蝇草是一种吃昆虫的食肉植物。 | carn->肉（拉丁caro），vor->吃，carnivorous->吃肉的（cf. carnage屠杀，carnival狂欢节=吃肉前的节日，incarnate肉身的）

quiz.question: ferrous _____
quiz.explanation: 含铁的, 含铁金属. Steel is a ferrous metal alloy containing iron. 钢是一种含铁的金属合金。 | ferr->铁（拉丁ferrum），ferrous->含铁的（cf. ferric铁的，ferrite铁素体，nonferrous有色金属=非铁金属）

quiz.question: lustrous ____
quiz.explanation: 有光泽的; 光亮的, 有光泽的头发. She maintained lustrous hair through careful grooming. 她通过精心打理保持头发有光泽。 | lustr->发光（拉丁lustrare），lustrous->发光的=有光泽的（cf. luster光泽，illustrious杰出的=被照亮的，lackluster无光泽）

quiz.question: ambidextrous _____
quiz.explanation: 双手都灵巧的, 双手灵巧的技能. His ambidextrous skill allowed him to write with either hand. 他双手灵巧的技能使他可以用任何一只手写字。 | ambi->两者，dexter->右手，ambidextrous->两手都像右手一样灵巧的（cf. dexterous灵巧，sinister左/邪恶）

quiz.question: felicitous ______
quiz.explanation: 恰当的; 得体的, 恰当的措辞. The writer chose a felicitous phrase to express the idea. 作家选择了一个恰当的措辞来表达这个想法。 | felix->幸运/快乐（拉丁），felicitous->恰到好处带来愉悦的（cf. felicity幸福，Felix姓名，infelicitous不恰当的）

quiz.question: infelicitous ______
quiz.explanation: 不恰当的; 不得体的, 不恰当的评论. His infelicitous remark offended several guests. 他不恰当的评论冒犯了几位客人。 | in->否定，felix->幸运，infelicitous->不恰当带来不快的（cf. felicitous恰当的，infelicity不幸/不恰当）

quiz.question: solicitous ____
quiz.explanation: 关心的; 体贴的, 体贴的照顾. The nurse provided solicitous care to all patients. 护士为所有病人提供了体贴的照顾。 | sol->全部，cit->激动（拉丁），solicitous->全心牵挂的=体贴关心的（cf. solicit请求，solicitor律师，solicitous关怀备至）

quiz.question: duplicitous _______
quiz.explanation: 欺骗的; 两面派的, 欺骗的行为. His duplicitous conduct betrayed his friends trust. 他欺骗的行为背叛了朋友的信任。 | duplic->两层（拉丁duplex->双重），duplicitous->两面三刀的（cf. duplicate复制，duplex双重的，duplicity两面派）

quiz.question: necessitous ______
quiz.explanation: 贫困的; 贫穷的, 贫困的家庭. The charity helps necessitous family in need. 慈善机构帮助需要帮助的贫困家庭。 | necess->必需（拉丁necesse），necessitous->缺乏必需品的=贫困的（cf. necessary必要的，necessity必需品，necesse不可避免）

quiz.question: circuitous _____
quiz.explanation: 迂回的; 绕道的, 迂回的路线. They took a circuitous route to avoid the traffic jam. 他们走了一条迂回的路线以避开交通堵塞。 | circuit->绕圈（拉丁circuire->绕行），circuitous->绕道而行的=迂回的（cf. circle圆，circuit巡回，circumference周长）

quiz.question: iniquitous ______
quiz.explanation: 不公正的; 邪恶的, 不公正的政策. The government abolished the iniquitous policy. 政府废除了不公正的政策。 | in->否定，equ->平等（拉丁aequus），iniquitous->不平等不公正的（cf. equal平等，equity公平，inequity不公平，iniquity邪恶）

quiz.question: gratuitous ______
quiz.explanation: 无端的; 无理由的, 无端的侮辱. His gratuitous insult was completely uncalled for. 他无端的侮辱完全是无缘无故的。 | gratu->感激/免费（拉丁gratia->恩惠），gratuitous->无需付出的=无偿的/无端的（cf. gratitude感激，gratuity小费，gratis免费）

quiz.question: fortuitous _________
quiz.explanation: 偶然的; 意外的, 偶然的相遇. Their fortuitous encounter led to a lasting friendship. 他们偶然的相遇导致了持久的友谊。 | fortuit->偶然（拉丁fortuna->命运），fortuitous->命运偶然安排的（cf. fortune命运，fortunate幸运的，fortuitous偶然但通常是好的）

quiz.question: momentous ________
quiz.explanation: 重大的; 重要的, 重大的决定. The board made a momentous decision to merge. 董事会做出了合并的重大决定。 | moment->瞬间/重要（拉丁momentum），momentous->具有巨大重量/重要性的（cf. moment时刻，momentum动量，momentary短暂的）

quiz.question: portentous ____
quiz.explanation: 预兆的; 不祥的, 不祥的征兆. Dark clouds were a portentous sign of the coming storm. 乌云是即将来临的暴风雨的不祥征兆。 | portent->预兆（拉丁portendere->预示），portentous->充满不祥预兆的（cf. portent凶兆，portend预示，omen征兆）

quiz.question: riotous _____
quiz.explanation: 骚乱的; 喧闹的, 骚乱的人群. Police dispersed the riotous crowd with tear gas. 警察用催泪瓦斯驱散了骚乱的人群。 | riot->骚乱（古法语riote->争吵），riotous->骚乱的/狂欢的（cf. riot暴乱，riotous living放荡生活，riotous applause热烈掌声）

quiz.question: vacuous ______
quiz.explanation: 空洞的; 无意义的, 空洞的评论. His vacuous remark added nothing to the discussion. 他空洞的评论对讨论毫无帮助。 | vacu->空（拉丁vacuus），vacuous->空洞无物的（cf. vacuum真空，evacuate撤离=清空，vacant空缺，vacation假期=空闲）

quiz.question: conspicuous _______
quiz.explanation: 显眼的; 显著的, 显著的成功. The project was a conspicuous success for the team. 该项目对团队来说是显著的成功。 | con->加强，spic->看，conspicuous->非常显眼的（cf. perspicuous清晰，perspicacious洞察力强，inspect检查）

quiz.question: perspicuous _____
quiz.explanation: 明晰的; 清楚的, 明晰的风格. The author wrote in a perspicuous style easy to understand. 作者以易于理解的明晰风格写作。 | per->穿透，spic->看，perspicuous->能看穿的=清晰明白的（cf. perspicacious洞察力强，conspicuous显眼，opaque不透明）

quiz.question: innocuous ______

quiz.explanation: 步态; 步法, 笨拙的步态. He walked with an awkward gait after the injury. 受伤后他走路的步态很笨拙。 | gate->道路（古挪威gat->通道），gait->走路的方式=步态（cf. gate大门，gait步态，注意gate/gait同音）

quiz.question: _______ trait
quiz.explanation: 特征; 特性, 遗传特征. Eye color is determined by genetic trait. 眼睛颜色由遗传特征决定。 | trait->拉出（法语traire->拉），trait->拉出来显现的特征（cf. tract大片，portrait肖像=向前画，betray出卖）

quiz.question: distrait ______
quiz.explanation: 心不在焉的, 心不在焉的态度. She answered in a distrait manner, clearly preoccupied. 她以心不在焉的态度回答,显然心事重重。 | dis->分开，trait->拉，distrait->注意力被拉走的=心不在焉的（cf. distract分心，distraught极度烦恼，absent-minded）

quiz.question: _ bit ____
quiz.explanation: 一点; 少量, 一个小角色. He played a bit part in the movie. 他在电影中扮演了一个小角色。 | bit->一小块（古英语bita->咬的一口），bit part->极小的角色（cf. bit钻头/比特/一点，bite咬，a bit有点）

quiz.question: inhabit _____
quiz.explanation: 居住; 栖息, 居住在洞穴. Ancient peoples used to inhabit caves. 古代人曾经居住在洞穴中。 | in->在内，habit->居住（拉丁habitare），inhabit->在里面居住=栖息（cf. habitat栖息地，habit习惯，cohabit同居）

quiz.question: cohabit ____
quiz.explanation: 同居, 与...同居. The couple chose to cohabit with each other before marriage. 这对夫妇选择在婚前与对方同居。 | co->共同，habit->居住，cohabit->共同居住=同居（cf. inhabit居住，habitat栖息地，habitual习惯的）

quiz.question: inhibit ______
quiz.explanation: 抑制; 阻止, 抑制生长. Lack of sunlight will inhibit growth of plants. 缺乏阳光会抑制植物的生长。 | in->进入/阻止，hibit->持有（拉丁habere），inhibit->阻止持续=抑制（cf. exhibit展示，prohibit禁止，inhibition抑制）

quiz.question: tacit _______
quiz.explanation: 心照不宣的; 默许的, 默许. Their tacit consent was understood without words. 他们的默许无需言语就能理解。 | tacit->沉默（拉丁tacere->沉默），tacit->不言而喻的=心照不宣（cf. taciturn沉默寡言的，reticent沉默的）

quiz.question: ______ deficit
quiz.explanation: 赤字; 不足, 预算赤字. The government faces a large budget deficit. 政府面临巨大的预算赤字。 | de->向下，ficit->做（拉丁facere），deficit->做不够=不足/赤字（cf. defect缺陷，efficient有效，suffice足够）

quiz.question: licit _____
quiz.explanation: 合法的, 合法贸易. The business engaged in licit trade activities. 该企业从事合法贸易活动。 | licet->被允许（拉丁），licit->合法允许的（cf. illicit非法，elicit引出，solicit征求，license许可）

quiz.question: elicit ________

quiz.question: illicit _____

quiz.question: solicit _________

quiz.question: implicit ____
quiz.explanation: 含蓄的; 隐含的, 隐含偏见. Everyone has some form of implicit bias. 每个人都有某种形式的隐含偏见。 | im->进入，plic->折叠（拉丁），implicit->折叠进去隐含的（cf. explicit明确展开的，implicate牵连，imply暗示）

quiz.question: explicit ____________
quiz.explanation: 明确的; 清楚的, 明确的指示. Follow the explicit instructions carefully. 仔细遵循明确的指示。 | ex->向外，plic->折叠，explicit->折叠展开=明确的（cf. implicit隐含，implicate牵连，explain解释=展开）

quiz.question: ___ plaudit
quiz.explanation: 赞扬; 喝彩, 赢得赞扬. The performance will win plaudit from critics. 这场演出将赢得评论家的赞扬。 | plaud->鼓掌（拉丁plaudere），plaudit->鼓掌喝彩=赞扬（cf. applaud鼓掌，applause掌声，explode爆炸=originally=鼓掌轰下台）

quiz.question: albeit _____

quiz.question: ___ __ deceit
quiz.explanation: 欺骗; 诡计, 欺骗行为. The lie was an act of deceit. 这个谎言是欺骗行为。 | de->向下，ceit->抓取（拉丁capere），deceit->向下抓取=欺骗（cf. receive接收，perceive感知，deceive欺骗=向下拉）

quiz.question: ______ conceit
quiz.explanation: 自负; 巧妙的比喻, 诗意的比喻. The poem uses an elaborate poetic conceit. 这首诗使用了一个精心设计的诗意比喻。 | con->共同，ceit->抓取/构思，conceit->共同构思=自负/文学巧喻（cf. conceive构思，receipt收据，perceive感知）

quiz.question: counterfeit ____
quiz.explanation: 伪造的; 假冒的, 伪钞. Police seized the counterfeit bill during the raid. 警方在突袭中缴获了伪钞。 | counter->对抗，feit->做（拉丁facere），counterfeit->反向做=伪造的（cf. perfect完美，deficit赤字，forfeit没收）

quiz.question: surfeit __ ____
quiz.explanation: 过量; 过度, 过量食物. The feast provided a surfeit of food. 宴会提供了过量的食物。 | sur->超过，feit->做，surfeit->过度做/过量（cf. surplus过剩，surpass超过，surfeit vs. excess同义）

quiz.question: discomfit _____
quiz.explanation: 使困惑; 使挫败, 挫败敌人. The strategy will discomfit enemy forces. 这个策略将挫败敌军。 | dis->否定，comfit->舒适/打败（古法语confire->击败），discomfit->使不舒适/挫败（注意：discomfit≠discomfort，更强烈）

quiz.question: ___ _ whit
quiz.explanation: 一点; 少许, 一点也不. He cared not a whit about the consequences. 他对后果一点也不在乎。 | whit->极小的量（古英语wiht->东西），not a whit->连一点点都不（cf. iota一点点=希腊字母最小，jot一点）

quiz.question: _____ skit
quiz.explanation: 幽默短剧; 滑稽表演, 喜剧小品. The actors performed a comic skit on stage. 演员们在舞台上表演了一个喜剧小品。 | skit->讽刺短剧（古挪威skjota->射/快速移动），skit->讽刺小品（cf. sketch素描/速写小品，skit轻松讽刺）

quiz.question: split _____
quiz.explanation: 分裂; 劈开, 吹毛求疵. Do not split hairs over minor details. 不要在小细节上吹毛求疵。 | split->劈开（荷兰语splitten），split hairs->把毛发都劈开=吹毛求疵（cf. cleave劈，split decision分歧裁决）

quiz.question: remit _______
quiz.explanation: 汇款; 宽恕, 汇款. Please remit payment within 30 days. 请在30天内汇款。 | re->回，mit->送（拉丁mittere），remit->送回/汇款（cf. submit提交，commit承诺，transmit传输，emit发射）

quiz.question: delimit ___ _____
quiz.explanation: 划定界限, 界定范围. We need to delimit the scope of the project. 我们需要界定项目的范围。 | de->彻底，limit->限制，delimit->划清界限=界定（cf. limit限制，unlimited无限，delimiter分隔符）

quiz.question: ________ hermit
quiz.explanation: 隐士, 山中隐士. The mountain hermit lived alone for decades. 山中隐士独自生活了几十年。 | eremite->荒野居民（希腊eremos->荒漠），hermit->在荒野独居的人=隐士（cf. hermitage隐居处，hermetically密封=隐士式封闭）

quiz.question: transmit ____

quiz.question: manumit ______
quiz.explanation: 解放(奴隶), 解放奴隶. The owner decided to manumit slaves before the law required it. 主人决定在法律要求之前解放奴隶。 | manu->手，mit->送，manumit->从手中放开送走=解放奴隶（cf. manual手动，manufacture制造，manuscript手稿）

quiz.question: knit _ _______
quiz.explanation: 编织, 编织毛衣. She learned to knit a sweater for her grandson. 她学会了为孙子编织毛衣。 | knit->编织（古英语cnyttan->打结），knit->编织/紧密结合（cf. knot结，net网，knit one's brows皱眉）

quiz.question: exploit ___________
quiz.explanation: 开发; 利用, 利用机会. Smart businesses exploit opportunity when it arises. 聪明的企业会在机会出现时加以利用。 | ex->向外，ploit->折叠（拉丁plicare），exploit->展开利用（cf. exploit功绩，implicit隐含，explicit明确，exploit可褒可贬）

quiz.question: adroit __________
